skill.name: imaginify-guide
skill.description: Guides users through Imaginify features, tutorials, best practices, and developer positioning for the Imaginify AI image platform.
skill.summary: Provides concise Imaginify tutorials, feature overviews, and usage guidance.
skill.author: ImaginifyAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs, shell backtick execution, and weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found the URLs are documentation and product links, the backticks are Markdown formatting, and no executable code or cryptographic operation is present. The remaining concern is minor promotional linking to an external website.

low_findings.title: External Product Links in Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill repeatedly references https://imaginify.app in its guidance and examples. This is a hardcoded external website, but no code requests, automatic browsing, credential transfer, or data exfiltration were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are directly visible in the reviewed files. The context shows product guidance and examples, not automatic network execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive Markdown Command Formatting
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the reviewed lines use Markdown inline code formatting around names and URLs. No script file, shell invocation, subprocess call, or executable command block was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown prose. There is no Ruby source, shell syntax context, or runtime path for command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns on prose lines. Manual review found platform description and feature text only, with no hashing, encryption, token handling, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain natural-language documentation. No cryptographic operation or security-sensitive data handling is present.

content.value_statement: New users can struggle to understand which Imaginify tool fits their image workflow. This skill provides concise tutorials, feature explanations, and practical best practices.
actual_capabilities: Explains what Imaginify is and where to start.
Summarizes documented image tools such as text-to-image and background removal.
Provides step-by-step guidance for common Imaginify workflows.
Shares prompt and image-quality tips from the skill documentation.
Describes the stated Next.js SaaS boilerplate positioning for developers.
limitations: Does not connect to Imaginify or perform actions inside a user account.
Does not verify current pricing, limits, or feature availability.
Does not generate or edit images by itself.
Relies on the documentation included in the skill files.

use_cases.title: Start using Imaginify
use_cases.description: Get a plain-language overview of the platform and its main image tools.

use_cases.title: Improve creative workflows
use_cases.description: Learn practical tips for prompts, background removal, restoration, and image previews.

use_cases.title: Assess the SaaS boilerplate
use_cases.description: Understand the documented developer positioning and technology stack at a high level.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Imaginify
prompt_templates.prompt: What is Imaginify, and which image tools should I try first?

prompt_templates.title: Guide a Background Removal Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk me through using Imaginify to remove a photo background, including best-practice tips.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Image Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a detailed prompt for Imaginify text-to-image generation for a product concept.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Developer Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess whether Imaginify sounds suitable as a starting point for an AI SaaS product.

output_examples.output: A concise overview that explains the platform, names several image tools, and directs the user to the official site.

output_examples.input: How should I use the background remover?
output: A short sequence of account, upload, process, preview, and download steps.
Practical advice to use images with clear subject edges.

output_examples.output: A high-level explanation of its stated Next.js, React, Drizzle ORM, and Better Auth boilerplate positioning.

best_practices: Ask for the specific Imaginify tool or workflow you want to understand.
Provide image goals, style preferences, and quality needs when requesting prompt help.
Verify pricing, limits, and production details on the official site before making decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not assume the skill can access your Imaginify account or files.
Do not rely on it for current pricing or legal product commitments.
Do not use vague creative requests when you need precise prompt guidance.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It explains Imaginify features, tutorials, best practices, and developer positioning from the included documentation.

faq.question: Can it generate images for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance only and does not run image generation or editing tools.

faq.question: Does it connect to Imaginify?
faq.answer: No. It references the Imaginify website but does not make authenticated account changes.

faq.question: Which tools are covered?
faq.answer: The skill describes text-to-image, image-to-image, background removal, photo restoration, and background blur.

faq.question: Is it useful for developers?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize the documented SaaS boilerplate positioning and listed technology stack.

faq.question: Should I verify details elsewhere?
faq.answer: Yes. Check the official Imaginify site for current pricing, limits, and available features.
